rationale: This is a canonical, textbook example of the Wolff-Kishner reduction. It uses a simple, acyclic, aliphatic ketone (3-pentanone) and reduces it to the corresponding alkane (n-pentane). The reagents, hydrazine (NN) and potassium hydroxide ([OH-].[K+]), are the classic conditions for this transformation. The structure is simple, RDKit-parseable, and clearly demonstrates the conversion of a carbonyl group to a methylene group.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses a simple aliphatic ketone to avoid overlap with reactions involving aromatic rings (e.g., Friedel-Crafts acylation/alkylation) or other functional groups. It specifically avoids aldehydes, which could be involved in aldol or Cannizzaro reactions, and avoids cyclic ketones which might be involved in ring expansions (e.g., Tiffeneau-Demjanov) or other ring-specific chemistry. The simplicity focuses squarely on the C=O to CH2 transformation characteristic of the Wolff-Kishner reduction.
